enrichment.short_description: AI-powered, privacy-first, self-hosted app for browsing, organizing, and sharing photos and videos with face recognition and smart search.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Self-hosted, AI-powered photo and video manager with face recognition, smart search, and privacy-first design. Free community edition available.

features.title: Face Recognition
features.description: Automatically recognizes and groups faces of family and friends across your photo library.

features.title: AI Content Labeling
features.description: Classifies photos automatically based on subject matter and location data.

features.title: Advanced Search
features.description: Filter photos by label, location, resolution, color, and quality.

features.title: RAW & Video Support
features.description: Handles RAW image formats and multiple video formats without manual conversion.

features.title: Interactive World Maps
features.description: Six high-resolution maps for visualizing travel memories using privacy-preserving geocoding.

features.title: Live Photos Playback
features.description: View Live Photos with hover previews and slideshow support.

features.title: WebDAV Sync
features.description: Connect via a WebDAV client or compatible backup apps like PhotoSync for automatic phone backup.

pros: Free, self-hosted Community Edition with unlimited storage for personal use
Strong AI-powered face recognition and automatic content classification
Privacy-first: data stays under user control, with no third-party data sharing
Supports RAW images and a wide range of video formats
Managed hosting available via PikaPods for users who don't want to self-host
cons: Essentials and Plus editions are paid monthly even though they are still self-hosted
Requires Docker or similar technical setup for self-hosting
Documentation does not describe native desktop or mobile apps beyond the web/PWA interface

faqs.question: Is PhotoPrism free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and open source (AGPL 3.0) with unlimited storage for personal use. Paid Essentials and Plus editions add features like additional user roles and enhanced geocoding.

faqs.question: What license does PhotoPrism use?
faqs.answer: PhotoPrism is released under the AGPL 3.0 license, with documentation available under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

faqs.question: Can I use PhotoPrism without self-hosting it myself?
faqs.answer: Yes, fully managed hosting is available through PikaPods starting from $6.50 per month.

faqs.question: Where is PhotoPrism based?
faqs.answer: PhotoPrism UG is based in Berlin, Germany, and describes itself as 100% self-funded and independent.

faqs.question: Does PhotoPrism support face recognition?
faqs.answer: Yes, it automatically recognizes the faces of family and friends across a photo library.

use_cases.title: Private photo archiving
use_cases.description: Self-host a personal photo and video library instead of relying on a commercial cloud service.

use_cases.title: Family photo organization
use_cases.description: Organize and share family photos using automatic face recognition and content classification.

use_cases.title: Team photo management
use_cases.description: Use the Essentials or Plus editions for multi-user roles and management features in small teams.

features: Unlimited storage and instances for personal use
Folder structure, duplicate detection, scheduled indexing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is PhotoPrism?
paragraphs: PhotoPrism is an AI-powered, privacy-first application for browsing, organizing, and sharing photos and videos. It is built and maintained by PhotoPrism UG, a self-funded, independent company based in Berlin, Germany, founded in 2018, and is designed to be self-hosted so users retain control over their media and data.

The Community Edition is free and open source under the AGPL 3.0 license, offering unlimited storage and instances for personal use. Paid Essentials and Plus editions add features such as additional user roles, enhanced geocoding, and 3D vector and satellite maps, while PikaPods offers fully managed hosting starting from $6.50 per month for users who prefer not to self-host.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who PhotoPrism is for
paragraphs: PhotoPrism is aimed at privacy-conscious individuals, families, and small teams who want an alternative to commercial cloud photo services without giving up AI-powered features like face recognition and automatic content classification.

Because it supports RAW image formats, multiple video formats, and deployment via Docker on hardware ranging from Raspberry Pi to NAS devices, it also suits home-lab and self-hosting enthusiasts who want control over their own infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source photo management service with AI-powered face recognition, object detection, and semantic search.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Open-source, self-hosted photo manager with face recognition, object detection, and semantic search powered by machine learning. Free and MIT licensed.

features.title: Face Recognition & Classification
features.description: Detects and groups faces using local machine learning models such as face_recognition and scikit-learn.

features.title: Object & Scene Detection
features.description: Automatically tags photos based on detected objects and scenes.

features.title: Semantic Search
features.description: Search the photo library using metadata-based, semantic queries.

features.title: Timeline View
features.description: Automatically organizes photos into a chronological timeline via scanning.

features.title: Event-Based Albums
features.description: Automatically generates albums grouped by events.

features.title: Multiuser Support
features.description: Multiple user accounts can access the same LibrePhotos instance.

features.title: Reverse Geocoding
features.description: Converts photo GPS data into readable location names.

features.title: Android App
features.description: Dedicated Android mobile client built with React Native.

pros: Completely free and open source under the MIT license
Advanced AI features (face recognition, object detection, semantic search) run locally
Actively maintained with regular releases, including version 1.0.3 in June 2026
Multiuser support for shared or family libraries
Dedicated Android mobile client available
cons: Requires Docker and self-hosting technical knowledge to run
No iOS client is documented
No official hosted or managed cloud option

faqs.question: Is LibrePhotos free?
faqs.answer: Yes, LibrePhotos is fully open source under the MIT license with no paid tiers documented.

faqs.question: Does LibrePhotos support face recognition?
faqs.answer: Yes, it uses machine learning tools including face_recognition and scikit-learn for face detection and clustering.

faqs.question: What platforms does LibrePhotos run on?
faqs.answer: It is deployed via Docker and Docker Compose, is recommended on Linux, and offers a React web client plus a React Native Android app.

faqs.question: Is LibrePhotos actively maintained?
faqs.answer: Yes, the project has ongoing releases, with version 1.0.3 released in June 2026.

faqs.question: Can multiple users share a LibrePhotos instance?
faqs.answer: Yes, LibrePhotos supports multiuser access to the same instance.

use_cases.title: Private family photo library
use_cases.description: Run a self-hosted alternative to commercial cloud photo services for family photo storage.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted media archiving
use_cases.description: Deploy via Docker for personal or homelab photo archiving with full data control.

use_cases.title: AI-powered offline photo search
use_cases.description: Use semantic search and object detection without sending images to a third-party cloud.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LibrePhotos?
paragraphs: LibrePhotos is a self-hosted, open-source photo management service with automatic face recognition, object detection, and semantic search, powered by machine learning models such as face_recognition, scikit-learn, and places365. It is released under the MIT license and is actively maintained, with version 1.0.3 released in June 2026.

The application supports all major photo types, including RAW images and videos, and organizes them into a timeline view with event-based albums. It uses a Django and PostgreSQL backend with a React/TypeScript frontend, and is deployed via Docker and Docker Compose.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LibrePhotos is for
paragraphs: LibrePhotos is built for individuals and families who want commercial-grade AI photo organization features, such as face recognition and semantic search, without sending their images to a third-party cloud service.
Its multiuser support and dedicated Android client also make it suitable for households or small groups sharing a self-hosted photo library.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source photo gallery software for self-hosted or cloud-hosted album management, sharing, and organization.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Open-source photo gallery for self-hosted or cloud album management, with plugins, themes, and permissions. Free software or paid cloud plans.

features.title: Albums Hierarchy
features.description: Organize photos into nested album structures.

features.title: Batch Management
features.description: Bulk edit, tag, and manage large photo collections at once.

features.title: Plugins & Themes
features.description: Extend functionality with more than 200 community plugins and themes.

features.title: Permissions Control
features.description: Set granular access permissions for albums and users.

features.title: High-Volume Photo Handling
features.description: Manages libraries ranging from thousands to hundreds of thousands of photos.

features.title: Multi-language Interface
features.description: Available in a dozen-plus languages, including English, French, German, and Spanish.

pros: Free, open-source self-hosted option under the GPL v2 license
Long track record, in continuous development since 2002
Highly extensible with more than 200 plugins and themes
Optional managed cloud hosting available via piwigo.com for non-technical users
Multi-language interface
cons: Cloud hosting plans on piwigo.com require annual billing
Self-hosted setup requires PHP/MySQL server administration knowledge
Features like AI tagging and full file-format support require higher-cost cloud plans (Enterprise and above)

faqs.question: Is Piwigo free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted software is free and open source under the GPL v2 license. Paid managed cloud hosting plans are also available via piwigo.com.

faqs.question: What are Piwigo's cloud pricing plans?
faqs.answer: Piwigo.com plans range from Pro (€29/month, 10 GB, annual billing) up to VIP (€399/month, 1,000 GB), all billed annually with a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: How long has Piwigo been around?
faqs.answer: Piwigo was founded in 2002 and has been in continuous development for over two decades.

faqs.question: Can I extend Piwigo's functionality?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are more than 200 plugins and themes available for Piwigo.

faqs.question: What languages does Piwigo support?
faqs.answer: Piwigo supports many languages, including English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Dutch, Polish, Portuguese (Brazilian), Turkish, Russian, Danish, and Simplified Chinese.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted photo sharing
use_cases.description: Deploy Piwigo on your own PHP/MySQL server for full control over a photo gallery.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud photo galleries
use_cases.description: Use piwigo.com's hosted plans to run a photo gallery without managing server infrastructure.

use_cases.title: High-volume photo archives
use_cases.description: Organize and manage very large photo collections using albums hierarchy and batch management.

features: All file formats (PSD, AI, EPS, PDF, etc.)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Piwigo?
paragraphs: Piwigo is open-source photo gallery software for organizing, managing, and sharing photos on the web, in continuous development since 2002. It is released under the GPL v2 license and can be self-hosted on a PHP/MySQL server, or used through piwigo.com's managed cloud hosting plans.

The software supports album hierarchies, batch photo management, and granular permissions, and can be extended with more than 200 community plugins and themes. It is available in a dozen or more languages, including English, French, German, and Spanish.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Piwigo is for
paragraphs: Piwigo suits organizations, teams, and individuals who need to manage large photo libraries, from thousands to hundreds of thousands of images, with fine-grained permission controls.
Its self-hosted option appeals to users comfortable managing their own PHP/MySQL server, while its piwigo.com cloud plans, which include a 30-day free trial, serve users who prefer managed hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted image and video hosting platform with a free open-source edition and paid Lite/Pro editions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chevereto is self-hosted image and video hosting software with a free open-source edition and paid Lite/Pro plans.

features.title: Media management
features.description: Organize images and videos with categories, tags, and albums.

features.title: Multi-user accounts
features.description: User registration, profiles, two-factor authentication, and role-based access control.

features.title: Multi-tenant support
features.description: Host multiple independent sites from a single Chevereto installation.

features.title: Multi-language interface
features.description: The platform is available in over 30 languages.

features.title: Privacy controls
features.description: Granular content visibility and privacy settings for uploaded media.

features.title: CDN and optimization
features.description: Built-in CDN support, caching, and image optimization.

features.title: API access
features.description: An extensive API is available for integrating Chevereto with other systems.

features.title: Advanced storage integrations
features.description: Pro edition connects to S3 and Cloudflare R2 for scalable storage.

pros: Free, open-source (AGPLv3) edition available
Self-hosted, giving full control over content and data
Supports over 30 languages
Extensive API for integrations
Actively maintained, with a July 2026 release (4.5.5)
cons: Advanced storage integrations (S3, Cloudflare R2) require the paid Pro edition
Self-hosting requires a webserver, PHP, and MySQL/MariaDB setup and maintenance
Paid editions require subscription renewal to keep receiving updates and support

faqs.question: Is Chevereto free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chevereto offers a free, open-source edition licensed under AGPLv3. Paid Lite and Pro editions are also available with additional features and support.

faqs.question: What licensing does Chevereto use?
faqs.answer: The free edition uses the AGPLv3 open-source license, while the Lite and Pro editions use a proprietary commercial license.

faqs.question: Can Chevereto be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chevereto is designed to be self-hosted on a webserver with PHP, MySQL or MariaDB, and optional Redis.

faqs.question: Does Chevereto support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chevereto supports over 30 languages.

faqs.question: How is Chevereto installed?
faqs.answer: It can be installed via Docker, VPS providers, cPanel, Plesk, or automated installers.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted media sharing community
use_cases.description: Build a Flickr- or Imgur-style platform for user-generated image and video uploads with full control over content and rules.

use_cases.title: Branded image hosting
use_cases.description: Host and manage branded media assets using the Pro edition's advanced S3/Cloudflare R2 storage integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Chevereto?
paragraphs: Chevereto is a self-hosted media-sharing platform that lets users build their own image and video hosting website, similar to Flickr or Imgur, with complete control over content, data, and policies.

It uses a dual-licensing model: a free edition released under the AGPLv3 open-source license, and paid Lite and Pro editions under a proprietary commercial license that add features like advanced storage integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Chevereto is for
paragraphs: Chevereto suits individuals and organizations that want to run their own branded image and video hosting service instead of relying on third-party platforms, particularly those comfortable self-hosting PHP/MySQL applications.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted personal photo and video manager with automatic deduplication and organization.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: PhotoStructure is a self-hosted photo and video manager with a free Lite tier and a paid Plus plan from $5/month.

features.title: Automatic organization
features.description: Organizes photos and videos into customizable folder structures on your own storage.

features.title: Deduplication
features.description: Groups duplicates, edited versions, and resized copies of the same photo or video.

features.title: Cross-platform, portable libraries
features.description: Libraries run on Windows, macOS, Linux, or Docker and remain portable across systems.

features.title: Broad format support
features.description: Supports many photo and video file formats.

features.title: Automatic video transcoding
features.description: Transcodes video files automatically for easier playback.

features.title: Keyword tagging and metadata handling
features.description: Tags assets with keywords and processes embedded metadata.

features.title: Face tag extraction
features.description: Extracts face tags from photos as part of the Plus plan.

features.title: Privacy-focused design
features.description: No tracking or analytics; files stay in their original, non-proprietary formats and locations.

pros: Free Lite tier available with no time limit
Self-hosted; files remain in original, non-proprietary formats and locations
No tracking or analytics
Designed to handle libraries with millions of files
Cross-platform: Windows, macOS, Linux, and Docker
cons: Advanced deduplication, mobile-friendly UI, and face tagging require the paid Plus subscription
New assets stop receiving automatic organization or tagging if a lapsed Plus subscription isn't renewed
Requires self-hosting and management on the user's own hardware

faqs.question: Is PhotoStructure free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Lite tier is free. A paid Plus plan ($5/month or $42/year) adds advanced deduplication, a mobile-friendly UI, and face tagging.

faqs.question: Does PhotoStructure require a subscription to keep my photos?
faqs.answer: No. PhotoStructure does not hold your library hostage — Lite access to your library remains free even without a Plus subscription.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Plus?
faqs.answer: Yes, Plus offers a 15-day free trial and does not require a credit card.

faqs.question: What platforms does PhotoStructure support?
faqs.answer: Windows, macOS, Linux, and Docker.

faqs.question: Is PhotoStructure self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it runs on user-controlled hardware and keeps files in their original formats and locations.

use_cases.title: Personal photo library organization
use_cases.description: Automatically organize and deduplicate a large personal collection of photos and videos stored on your own hardware.
use_cases.audience: Individuals with large personal photo or video archives

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious media management
use_cases.description: Manage a photo library without cloud uploads, tracking, or analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is PhotoStructure?
paragraphs: PhotoStructure is a self-hosted personal digital asset manager that organizes photos and videos on the user's own hardware, offering automatic deduplication, organization, and browsing across an entire library.
It uses a free-forever Lite tier alongside a paid Plus plan that unlocks advanced deduplication, automatic organization, face tag extraction, and a mobile-friendly UI.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who PhotoStructure is for
paragraphs: PhotoStructure fits people who want to keep large personal photo and video collections on their own storage rather than in the cloud, with privacy-focused, non-proprietary file handling.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, end-to-end encrypted photo storage app with a free 10GB plan and paid storage tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ente Photos is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted photo app with a free 10GB plan and paid storage from $2.49/month.

features.title: End-to-end encryption
features.description: Photos and metadata, including face detection and search indices, are end-to-end encrypted.

features.title: 3x data replication
features.description: Stored data is replicated three times for redundancy.

features.title: On-device face detection
features.description: Recognizes faces in photos to help organize a library.

features.title: Semantic search
features.description: Search photos by content rather than just filenames or tags.

features.title: Private sharing and collaborative albums
features.description: Share albums privately and collaborate with others.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: The full platform is open source and can be self-hosted on private infrastructure.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Available on iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, Linux, and the web.

features.title: Family plan
features.description: Paid subscribers can add up to five additional members to share a storage plan.

pros: Free 10GB storage plan available forever
Fully open source (AGPL-3.0) with the option to self-host
End-to-end encrypted, with audits by Cure53, Symbolic Software, and Fallible
Available on iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, Linux, and web
Family plan allows storage sharing among up to five additional members
cons: Paid subscription required for storage beyond the free 10GB
Self-hosting requires technical setup and ongoing infrastructure management

faqs.question: Is Ente Photos free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ente offers 10GB of free storage forever, with paid plans available for additional storage.

faqs.question: Is Ente open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ente is fully open source under the AGPL-3.0 license, and the entire platform can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: What platforms does Ente Photos support?
faqs.answer: iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, Linux, and web browsers.

faqs.question: How much do paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start around $2.49/month (billed annually, ~$2.99/month billed monthly) for 50GB, up to about $19.99/month (billed annually, ~$23.99/month billed monthly) for 2TB.

faqs.question: Does Ente offer family sharing?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid subscribers can add up to five additional members to share the storage on their plan.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused photo backup
use_cases.description: Store and back up personal photo libraries with end-to-end encryption instead of using Google Photos or iCloud.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted photo storage
use_cases.description: Deploy the open-source Ente stack on private infrastructure for complete data control.

use_cases.title: Family photo sharing
use_cases.description: Share encrypted storage and collaborative albums with family members under one paid plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ente Photos?
paragraphs: Ente Photos is a fully open-source, end-to-end encrypted photo and video storage app, positioned as an alternative to Google Photos and Apple Photos.

It offers 10GB of free storage forever, with paid plans for additional storage, 3x data replication, face detection, semantic search, and private, collaborative sharing. The full platform can also be self-hosted.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ente Photos is for
paragraphs: Ente suits individuals and families who want an encrypted, privacy-respecting alternative to mainstream cloud photo services, as well as technical users who prefer to self-host an open-source stack.

enrichment.short_description: Jellyfin is a free, open-source, self-hosted media server for streaming movies, TV, music, and photos.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jellyfin: a free, GPL-licensed, self-hosted media server for organizing and streaming your personal movie, TV, music, and photo library.

features.title: Self-hosted media server
features.description: Runs on your own hardware, giving you full control over your movie, TV, music, and photo library with no central servers collecting data.

features.title: Live TV and DVR
features.description: Supports live TV tuning and DVR recording alongside on-demand personal media.

features.title: SyncPlay
features.description: Allows synchronized playback so multiple users can watch the same content together remotely.

features.title: Books, comics, and photos
features.description: Manages additional media types beyond video and music, including books, comics, and photo collections.

features.title: Hardware-accelerated transcoding
features.description: Uses server GPU hardware acceleration to transcode media for playback on different devices.

features.title: Plugin extensibility
features.description: Supports community plugins to extend server functionality.

features.title: Broad client support
features.description: Official and third-party clients are available for web browsers, desktop, Android/Android TV, iOS/tvOS, Fire TV, Roku, and Kodi.

pros: Completely free with no premium tiers, hidden costs, or paywalled features
Fully open source under the GPL-2.0 license
No tracking or phone-home telemetry to central servers
Wide range of official and community clients across major platforms
Community-driven development with plugin extensibility
cons: Requires self-hosting; there is no managed/cloud-hosted option
Entirely volunteer-run, so support relies on community channels rather than a commercial help desk
As a fork of an older Emby release, some newer commercial-app polish found in paid competitors may be absent

faqs.question: Is Jellyfin free?
faqs.answer: Yes. Jellyfin states its server and official clients are free to download, now and always, with no hidden costs and no paid tier.

faqs.question: Is Jellyfin open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jellyfin is licensed under the GNU GPL-2.0 and developed entirely by volunteers.

faqs.question: Do I need my own hardware to run Jellyfin?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jellyfin operates exclusively as self-hosted software that you run on your own server or device.

faqs.question: Where did Jellyfin come from?
faqs.answer: Jellyfin began in December 2018 as a fork of Emby's 3.5.2 release, started after Emby moved its next release to a closed-source, proprietary model.

faqs.question: What kinds of media can Jellyfin manage?
faqs.answer: Jellyfin supports movies, TV shows, music, live TV with DVR, books, comics, and photos.

use_cases.title: Home media streaming
use_cases.description: Organize and stream a personal collection of movies, TV shows, and music to any device on or off the home network.
use_cases.audience: Home users with existing media libraries

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused self-hosting
use_cases.description: Run a media server with no central servers or tracking, keeping full control of data and infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Cost-free alternative to paid media servers
use_cases.description: Get media-server functionality comparable to commercial products without subscription costs.
use_cases.audience: Users seeking a free alternative to Emby or Plex

features: Server and official clients free to download and use
No hidden costs or paid tiers

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jellyfin?
paragraphs: Jellyfin is a free, open-source media system that lets users collect, organize, and stream their personal media libraries from a self-hosted server.
The project is licensed under the GNU GPL-2.0 and is developed entirely by volunteers, with no central servers collecting user data and no premium licenses or paid tiers.
Jellyfin began in December 2018 as a fork of Emby's 3.5.2 release, created after Emby announced its next major release would become closed-source.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jellyfin is for
paragraphs: Jellyfin suits home users and self-hosters who want to manage and stream movies, TV, music, live TV, books, comics, and photos from their own hardware without paying subscription or license fees.
It is also aimed at privacy-conscious users who prefer an open-source alternative to commercial media server products.

enrichment.short_description: Emby is a self-hosted personal media server with a free core tier and a paid Emby Premiere subscription for extra features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Emby is a self-hosted media server for movies, TV, music, and photos, with a free tier and paid Emby Premiere plans from $4.99/month.

features.title: Emby Connect
features.description: Enables remote access to your media server without manual IP address configuration.

features.title: Live TV and DVR
features.description: Supports live TV streaming and DVR recording management.

features.title: Parental controls
features.description: Offers access scheduling and time limits for managing content availability to users.

features.title: Automatic format conversion
features.description: Automatically converts and streams media on-the-fly to play on different devices.

features.title: DLNA support
features.description: Detects DLNA devices for content streaming and remote control.

features.title: Hardware-accelerated transcoding (Premiere)
features.description: Uses server GPU hardware acceleration to transcode video, available with Emby Premiere.

features.title: Offline downloads (Premiere)
features.description: Allows downloading media to smartphones and tablets for offline playback with Emby Premiere.

features.title: Cloud and folder sync (Premiere)
features.description: Emby Premiere adds cloud sync and folder sync to external drives, plus server backup and restore.

pros: Free core tier provides functional personal media server capabilities
Broad platform support across TVs, mobile, and computers
Emby Premiere lifetime option available as a one-time purchase instead of a subscription
Includes CarPlay, Android Auto, and smart-home voice control integration with Premiere
Live TV and DVR support built in
cons: Many advanced features (offline downloads, DVR, hardware transcoding, full app access) require the paid Emby Premiere tier
Requires self-hosting your own server rather than a fully managed cloud service
Premiere licensing is limited to single-household use with a 30-device cap

faqs.question: Is Emby free?
faqs.answer: Emby offers a free tier with core media server functionality; Emby Premiere is a separate paid subscription that adds extra features.

faqs.question: How much does Emby Premiere cost?
faqs.answer: Emby Premiere costs $4.99 per month, $54 for one year, or $119 as a one-time lifetime purchase.

faqs.question: What does Emby Premiere add?
faqs.answer: Premiere adds offline downloads, Emby DVR, full app access on Android/Fire TV/iOS/Xbox One/PS4, hardware-accelerated transcoding, Cinema Intros, CarPlay/Android Auto integration, cover art customization, and backup/restore.

faqs.question: Is Emby self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes. Users install Emby Server on their own system and connect to it through companion apps on other devices.

faqs.question: What platforms does Emby support?
faqs.answer: Emby supports Android TV, Apple TV, Google TV, Fire TV, Roku, LG, Samsung TVs, Android, iOS, Android Auto, Apple CarPlay, Windows, macOS, Linux, and web browsers.

use_cases.title: Home media library streaming
use_cases.description: Centralize home videos, music, and photos into one library accessible across devices.
use_cases.audience: Home users with personal media collections

use_cases.title: Live TV and DVR at home
use_cases.description: Stream and record live TV content through a self-hosted server.
use_cases.audience: Users who want DVR functionality via Emby Premiere

use_cases.title: Offline media on mobile
use_cases.description: Download media to phones and tablets for offline viewing using Emby Premiere.

features: Same features as monthly Premiere, billed yearly
One-time payment for lifetime Premiere access

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Emby?
paragraphs: Emby is a personal media management platform that automatically converts and streams media to play on virtually any device, centralizing home videos, music, and photos into a single library.

Emby follows a freemium model: a free tier covers core functionality, while the paid Emby Premiere subscription unlocks additional features such as offline downloads, DVR, hardware-accelerated transcoding, and expanded app access.
Emby operates on a self-hosted server architecture, where users install Emby Server locally and access their library through companion apps.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Emby is for
paragraphs: Emby suits users who want a self-hosted personal media server with the option to unlock extra features like DVR, offline downloads, and hardware transcoding through a paid subscription or one-time lifetime purchase.

enrichment.short_description: Plex is a self-hosted personal media server with a free tier and a paid Plex Pass subscription for advanced features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Plex is a personal media server for streaming movies, TV, and music, with a free tier and paid Plex Pass plans starting at $6.99/month.

features.title: Remote streaming
features.description: Stream your personal media library remotely, available to all users including on the free tier.

features.title: Hardware-accelerated transcoding (Plex Pass)
features.description: Uses dedicated video decoder/encoder hardware to transcode and stream HD or 4K video more smoothly to multiple devices at once.

features.title: Advanced parental controls (Plex Pass)
features.description: Lets server owners create customized restriction settings based on content ratings or custom labels for family and friends.

features.title: Skip Intro and Skip Credits (Plex Pass)
features.description: Automatically skips intros and end credits during playback.

features.title: Downloads (Plex Pass)
features.description: Allows downloading media for offline playback.

features.title: Plex DVR (Plex Pass)
features.description: Enables live TV recording through Plex DVR functionality.

pros: Free tier includes remote streaming of a personal media library
Plex Pass adds hardware transcoding, advanced parental controls, downloads, and DVR
Multiple Plex Pass billing options including monthly, annual, five-year, and lifetime
Existing lifetime Plex Pass holders keep their benefits even after price changes
cons: Many advanced features (hardware transcoding, parental controls, downloads, DVR) require a paid Plex Pass subscription
Lifetime Plex Pass price rose to $749.99 starting July 1, 2026, up from its prior price
Requires self-hosting your own Plex Media Server

faqs.question: Is Plex free?
faqs.answer: Plex offers a free tier that includes remote streaming of your personal media library; Plex Pass is a paid subscription that unlocks additional premium features.

faqs.question: How much does Plex Pass cost?
faqs.answer: Plex Pass costs $6.99 per month, $69.99 per year, $249.99 for a five-year plan, or $749.99 as a one-time lifetime purchase (lifetime pricing effective July 1, 2026).

faqs.question: What does Plex Pass include?
faqs.answer: Plex Pass adds hardware-accelerated transcoding, advanced parental controls, downloads for offline playback, Skip Intro and Skip Credits, and Plex DVR.

faqs.question: Did Plex Pass pricing recently change?
faqs.answer: Yes. The lifetime Plex Pass price increased to $749.99 starting July 1, 2026, while monthly and annual subscription pricing remained unchanged, and a new five-year plan was introduced at $249.99.

faqs.question: Is Plex self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Plex requires running your own Plex Media Server to host and stream your personal media library.

use_cases.title: Home media streaming
use_cases.description: Stream a personal library of movies, TV shows, and music remotely to devices on or off the home network.
use_cases.audience: Home users with personal media collections

use_cases.title: Live TV recording
use_cases.description: Record and manage live TV through Plex DVR with a Plex Pass subscription.
use_cases.audience: Plex Pass subscribers wanting DVR functionality

use_cases.title: Offline media access
use_cases.description: Download media for offline playback using Plex Pass.

features: Remote streaming of personal media library
Same features as monthly Plex Pass, billed yearly
Plex Pass features for a five-year term
One-time payment for lifetime Plex Pass access

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Plex?
paragraphs: Plex is a personal media server platform that lets users organize and stream their movies, TV shows, and music from a self-hosted server to devices on or off their home network.

Plex follows a freemium model: the free tier includes remote streaming, while the paid Plex Pass subscription unlocks features such as hardware-accelerated transcoding, advanced parental controls, downloads, Skip Intro and Credits, and Plex DVR.
Plex Pass is available as a monthly, annual, five-year, or one-time lifetime purchase, with lifetime pricing set at $749.99 as of July 1, 2026.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Plex is for
paragraphs: Plex suits users who want a self-hosted personal media server with an easy remote-streaming free tier, and the option to upgrade to Plex Pass for hardware transcoding, DVR, parental controls, and offline downloads.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, self-hosted music server that streams your personal music library from anywhere via a web app or Subsonic-compatible clients.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Navidrome is a free, open-source self-hosted music server with a web player, mobile app support, and Subsonic API compatibility.

features.title: Subsonic/OpenSubsonic API compatibility
features.description: Works with Subsonic, Madsonic, Airsonic, and other OpenSubsonic-compatible client apps on mobile and desktop.

features.title: Large library support
features.description: Handles very large music collections, tested with libraries of approximately 900,000 songs.

features.title: On-the-fly transcoding
features.description: Transcodes audio in real time, including Opus encoding, to reduce bandwidth and data usage.

features.title: Multi-user and multi-library
features.description: Supports multiple users with individual play counts, playlists, and favorites, plus multiple libraries with user-specific access controls.

features.title: Smart playlists
features.description: Generates dynamic, rule-based playlists similar to iTunes smart playlists.

features.title: Scrobbling integration
features.description: Integrates with Last.fm, ListenBrainz, and Maloja for scrobbling.

features.title: Automatic library monitoring
features.description: Detects changes to the music folder and updates the library automatically.

features.title: Lightweight footprint
features.description: Runs on resource-limited hardware such as Raspberry Pi Zero and old hardware setups.

pros: Completely free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license
Runs on minimal hardware, including Raspberry Pi
Scales to very large music libraries
Broad client compatibility via the Subsonic/OpenSubsonic API
Translated into 34 languages
cons: Requires self-hosting and basic server/Docker administration knowledge
No official managed cloud hosting from the Navidrome project itself
Relies on third-party Subsonic-compatible apps for mobile playback rather than a first-party official app for every platform

faqs.question: Is Navidrome free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Navidrome is distributed free of charge under the GNU GPL v3 license.

faqs.question: What platforms does Navidrome run on?
faqs.answer: Navidrome runs on macOS, Linux, and Windows, with official Docker images and Raspberry Pi binaries.

faqs.question: What client apps work with Navidrome?
faqs.answer: Navidrome supports a large selection of mobile and desktop clients through its Subsonic/OpenSubsonic API compatibility, including Subsonic, Madsonic, and Airsonic clients.

faqs.question: How large a music library can Navidrome handle?
faqs.answer: Navidrome has been tested with libraries of approximately 900,000 songs.

faqs.question: Does Navidrome support multiple users?
faqs.answer: Yes, Navidrome supports multiple users, each with their own play counts, playlists, and favorites, and library-level access controls.

use_cases.title: Personal music streaming
use_cases.description: Self-host a private streaming service to access a personal music collection from anywhere, similar to commercial streaming apps.
use_cases.audience: Individuals with large personal music libraries

use_cases.title: Home server / NAS music hosting
use_cases.description: Run Navidrome via Docker on low-power hardware like a Raspberry Pi or home NAS to serve music to household devices.

use_cases.title: Multi-user family music library
use_cases.description: Share one music collection among multiple family members, each with separate accounts, playlists, and play history.
use_cases.audience: Households sharing a single music archive

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Navidrome?
paragraphs: Navidrome is an open-source, self-hosted music collection server and streamer that lets users access their own music library from anywhere through a modern web interface and Subsonic-compatible mobile apps.
It is distributed free of charge under the GNU GPL v3 license, is lightweight enough to run on hardware such as a Raspberry Pi, and has been tested with libraries of roughly 900,000 songs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Navidrome is for
paragraphs: Navidrome is built for people who own large personal music collections and want a Spotify-like streaming experience for their own files, without relying on a commercial streaming service.

It suits self-hosters and home server users comfortable running Docker or native binaries on Linux, macOS, Windows, or a Raspberry Pi, and who want multi-user support with Subsonic/OpenSubsonic API client compatibility.
